0.solution: Uproot and destroy infected plants. Control the whitefly vector using systemic insecticides like Imidacloprid or Thiamethoxam. Use virus-resistant cotton varieties.

1.solution: Plant is healthy. Continue standard nutrient management (NPK), ensure proper spacing, and maintain regular scouting for pests.

2.solution: Plant is healthy. Maintain adequate soil moisture during the pod-filling stage. Prevent waterlogging.

3.solution: Citrus greening has no cure. Immediately remove infected trees to prevent spread. Control the Asian citrus psyllid vector using Dimethoate or Imidacloprid.

4.solution: Plant is healthy. Apply regular micronutrient sprays (Zinc, Iron) and ensure proper irrigation scheduling.

5.solution: Apply a fungicide such as Mancozeb or a Triazole (e.g., Propiconazole) at the first sign of pustules. Rotate crops to break the disease cycle.

6.solution: Plant is healthy. Provide adequate nitrogen side-dressing during the V6-V8 growth stages. Maintain weed control.

7.solution: Spray Mancozeb (0.2%) or Copper Oxychloride at 10-15 day intervals. Ensure early sowing and use certified, disease-free seeds.

8.solution: Plant is healthy. Apply a balanced dose of sulfur and phosphorus fertilizers for better pod formation and oil content.

9.solution: Critical: Apply protectant fungicides like Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb before infection. If infected, use curative systemic fungicides like Cymoxanil or Metalaxyl immediately.

10.solution: Plant is healthy. Practice regular crop rotation, earth up the soil around the base, and use certified seed tubers.

11.solution: Improve soil fertility (especially Nitrogen and Potassium). Spray crops with Mancozeb + Tricyclazole or Propiconazole at booting.

12.solution: Avoid excessive nitrogen application. Maintain a continuous flood in the paddy. Spray Tricyclazole or Isoprothiolane as soon as lesions appear.

13.solution: Plant is healthy. Maintain 2-5 cm of standing water and apply timely splits of nitrogen fertilizer.

14.solution: Highly destructive. Burn the infected crop immediately. Do not ratoon the diseased crop. Disinfect harvesting tools. Plant resistant varieties.

15.solution: Plant is healthy. Ensure adequate earthing up to prevent lodging. Manage timely irrigation and weed control.

16.solution: Remove lower infected leaves to improve airflow. Apply fungicides containing Chlorothalonil, Copper, or Mancozeb on a 7-14 day schedule.

17.solution: Plant is healthy. Stake or trellis the plants to keep foliage off the ground and avoid overhead watering to prevent future fungal issues.

18.solution: Spray Propiconazole (Tilt 25 EC) or Tebuconazole immediately upon spotting the first yellow stripes. Avoid excess nitrogen and late sowing.

19.solution: Plant is healthy. Ensure weed control (using appropriate herbicides if necessary) and provide adequate irrigation at the critical crown root initiation (CRI) stage.
